entries.title: Commentary on Lamentations on lamentations 3:10
entries.excerpt:
The manner for afflicting is stated here. First the manner is insidious, second, it is open.

Regarding the insidious manner for afflicting two more ideas are presented. First is the insidious action of enemies. "He is to me like a bear lying in wait, like a lion in hiding." Just like Nabuchodonosor fighting against me (Jeremiah), from ambush.

"He is to me like a bear": cruel, and "like a lion": in which is designated the above ruler (Nabuchodonosor). As the prophet Hosea relates: "So I will be to them like a lion, like a leopard I will lurk beside the way" (Hosea 13:7).
